formula_note: Core civilian direct-death series uses the MSF first-month violent-death floor for Rohingya victims.
uncertainty_note: This is a conservative floor-like estimate; the true first-month direct-death toll may be higher, but a tighter source-grounded upper bound was not identified for the same window.
quality_note: Best available direct-death floor for the acute phase.

formula_note: Auxiliary side-specific civilian series for the 25 August Kha Maung Seik massacre cluster.
uncertainty_note: Kept separate from the Rohingya-targeted civilian series because the victim population and denominator base differ.

formula_note: Strict-core military series counts only the official 13 security-force deaths and excludes status-contested alleged ARSA dead.
uncertainty_note: This almost certainly understates all combatant deaths in the episode, but it avoids folding ambiguous 'militant' bodies into the core military numerator.
quality_note: Solid for confirmed state-side military victims; incomplete for all combatants.

formula_note: Unknown-status deaths preserve official claims about insurgent/ARSA bodies without accepting them into the strict-core military series.
uncertainty_note: Status is the single largest uncertainty driver: some victims may have been trained fighters, some mobilized villagers, and some potentially misclassified civilians.
quality_note: Necessary bookkeeping row for unresolved status attribution.

formula_note: Baseline exposed Rohingya population = 233,826 remained/IDP + 665,101 in Bangladesh + 644 missing = 899,571. Person-months are bounded from cumulative outflow checkpoints at day 18 (313,000) and day 30 (430,000): low/high assume maximally front-loaded/back-loaded monotonic flight within the intervals; best assumes piecewise linear flight.
uncertainty_note: I did not add the 7,803 mixed-death series back into the baseline because it includes nonviolent deaths and extends beyond the acute one-month direct-death window; this makes the civilian denominator slightly conservative for the strict core.
quality_note: Good civilian exposure estimate for a fast-moving atrocity episode.

formula_note: One-month strict-core military exposure uses ICC's estimate of up to 11,000 soldiers plus 900 police involved in the 2017 wave.
uncertainty_note: This assumes those forces were relevantly assigned to the acute one-month theater; it still excludes ARSA because the strict core also excludes status-contested ARSA deaths.
quality_note: Strongest available deployment anchor without inflating to whole-country force size.

formula_note: DR = (military direct deaths / military person-months) / (civilian direct deaths / civilian person-months), using the strict-core military series (13 confirmed security-force deaths only).
uncertainty_note: Baseline strict DR is far below 1 (0.088-0.1263). This should be read as a lower-bound DR because status-contested alleged ARSA dead are excluded rather than forced into the military numerator.
quality_note: Best-supported baseline DR for the acute episode.

formula_note: Case-specific sensitivity counts 13 security-force deaths plus the 59 insurgents reportedly killed in the initial 25 August outpost assaults as organized-combatant deaths.
uncertainty_note: This sensitivity deliberately stops short of accepting the later official 376-body claim into the military series.
quality_note: Plausible narrow sensitivity tied to direct armed clashes.

formula_note: Case-specific military exposure adds an ARSA core-fighter range (200/300/500) to the 11,900 state-force denominator.
uncertainty_note: Amnesty's 'in the hundreds' wording is only strong enough for sensitivity analysis, not for the baseline core denominator.
quality_note: Reasonable sensitivity, but too inferential for the baseline.

formula_note: Case-specific sensitivity DR counts only the 59 insurgents killed in the actual 25 August assaults as combatants and adds a modest ARSA core-fighter denominator.
uncertainty_note: Case-specific sensitivity raises DR to about 0.4678-0.6877. If all 376 official ARSA bodies were accepted as combatants, an official-maximum sensitivity would rise to roughly 2.53-3.72, but that path is not adopted because the status evidence is too weak.
quality_note: Useful for showing how combatant-status choices move the ratio.
